title: Codex OAuth Eve Model Provider Tasks
principles: Prove the Codex-authenticated runtime path before replacing the current AI Gateway Eve model.
Keep Codex OAuth separate from app login, WorkOS, Better Auth, channels, and Eve tool contracts.
Use Effect Schema, tagged errors, Context services, Layers, Config, and KeyValueStore for every runtime-owned boundary.
Use Effect v4 local import paths for KeyValueStore while documenting the public Effect platform concept.
Treat OpenClaw as prior art only; do not adopt OpenClaw packages, code, state files, or gateway behavior.
Never commit, print, snapshot, or persist raw token values outside the approved token store.
globalVerification: Keep the completed personal executor DeepWiki and Parallel Task findings in the spec current before implementation starts.
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-oauth test when the package exists
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-oauth build when the package exists
bun run --filter @bundjil/agent test when app wiring changes
bun run --filter @bundjil/agent build when app wiring changes
Opt-in live proof only with local/private credentials and sanitized output

implementationImprovementAuditCounter.description: Before accepting each task, run parent audit passes for ownership/call graph, Effect implementation quality, and verification coverage. Require more passes if Codex auth support, token storage, Eve model-provider wiring, or secret handling remains weak.

tasks.title: Complete executor and DeepWiki research
tasks.description: Authorize executor personal, query DeepWiki for Codex OAuth, Eve model-provider wiring, and effect-smol KeyValueStore, then revise the spec with confirmed findings before code starts.
implementationNotes: Current attempt on 2026-07-07 is blocked on executor-personal OAuth authorization; `codex mcp login executor-personal` opened the browser authorization URL but timed out waiting for the loopback callback.

After the forked thread and successful executor-personal OAuth, `mcp__executor_personal.skills({ name: "execute" })` worked and DeepWiki was discovered at `deepwiki_mcp.user.personaldeepwikimcp.ask_question`.

DeepWiki on `vercel/eve` confirmed `agent.ts` can use a Gateway model string or any AI SDK `LanguageModel`; direct `LanguageModel` providers bypass Gateway attribution/caching and route through their own provider/modelId.

DeepWiki on `openai/codex` confirmed Codex auth modes include ChatGPT OAuth and access tokens, but `ChatgptAuthTokens` are marked unstable/internal. Current evidence does not support using Codex OAuth as arbitrary Eve chat-completion access.

DeepWiki on `Effect-TS/effect` confirmed the KeyValueStore concept, memory/file layers, schema-backed storage, and typed error mapping, while local Effect v4 source remains authoritative for the `effect/unstable/persistence/KeyValueStore` import path.

Parallel Task run `trun_e43fa758cfba40c3bf5ad5cddb1b7196` corrected the implementation direction: subscription-backed model access is viable through Codex app-server or a pinned Codex-to-OpenAI-compatible bridge, not by treating OAuth tokens as OpenAI API keys.
The full Parallel AI report is committed at docs/product-specs/codex-oauth-subscription-model-access.parallel-research.md.
The spec now treats `codex app-server` embedding with isolated CODEX_HOME as the preferred proof target and keeps Eve on AI Gateway until that path is proven.
mandatoryVerification: Executor personal OAuth is authorized or a fresh blocker is recorded with command output.
DeepWiki evidence is added to docs/product-specs/codex-oauth-eve-model-provider.md.
Flat meaningful Effect.gen programs are explicitly required for later primary operations.
Typed errors handled in .pipe(...) with catchTag, catchTags, or mapError are explicitly required.
Canonical Effect Schema contracts and schema-derived types from owning packages are explicitly required.
No unsafe casts, local DTO mirrors, manual object readers/mappers, or trivial wrappers/helpers are approved by the revised spec.
Implementation-improvement audit pass evidence is recorded before task acceptance.
completionCriteria: The spec names the chosen first runtime path or explicitly blocks implementation if no supported path exists.
The spec distinguishes official OpenAI/Codex support from OpenClaw prior art.
The task ledger is updated with research evidence and audit notes.

evidence: Pass 1 - ownership/call graph: DeepWiki and Parallel evidence kept implementation gated. Eve remains app-owned and Gateway-backed by default; Codex app-server/runtime becomes a separate prospective package/service boundary.

Pass 2 - Effect implementation quality: Research task tightened future implementation requirements around Effect KeyValueStore, Schema-backed profiles, app-server process/client services, tagged errors, local v4 import paths, and API-key env scrubbing without adding code.

Pass 3 - verification coverage: Executor personal OAuth succeeded in the forked thread, DeepWiki returned results for `vercel/eve`, `openai/codex`, and `Effect-TS/effect`, Parallel Task returned Codex subscription/app-server findings, and the spec/task ledger were updated with the findings.

tasks.title: Define @bundjil/codex-oauth schemas and services
tasks.description: Create the package contract with canonical schemas, tagged errors, service tags, storage keys, and memory layers without provider network calls.
implementationNotes: Use package layout src/schemas.ts, src/errors.ts, src/storage-keys.ts, src/profile-store.service.ts, src/token.service.ts, src/live.layer.ts, src/mock.layer.ts, and src/index.ts.
Use effect/unstable/persistence/KeyValueStore for local Effect v4.
Define OAuthProfileStore and CodexOAuthTokenService as the first stable service tags.
Use hashed subject keys under bundjil/oauth/v1/provider/codex/profile/{subjectHash}.
Do not add OAuth endpoint exchange or Eve model replacement in this task.
mandatoryVerification: Flat meaningful Effect.gen programs for primary operations.
Typed errors handled in .pipe(...) with catchTag, catchTags, or mapError where relevant.
Canonical Effect Schema contracts and schema-derived types from @bundjil/codex-oauth.
No unsafe casts, local DTO mirrors, manual object readers/mappers, or trivial wrappers/helpers.
Tests cover schema decode/encode, stable key derivation, missing profile, expired token, remove/revoke, storage error mapping, memory layer seeding, and no token leakage in error messages.
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-oauth test
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-oauth build
bun run check-types
completionCriteria: @bundjil/codex-oauth exports schemas, errors, service tags, and memory/live layers.
Package README documents contracts, layers, and safe secret handling.
No provider network dependency or Eve app model change is introduced.

tasks.title: Prove the Codex app-server subscription path
tasks.description: Run an opt-in local proof that Codex subscription auth can produce a model result through `codex app-server` or a private pinned bridge without exposing secrets.
implementationNotes: Prefer spawning `codex app-server` with isolated CODEX_HOME as the first proof target.
When subscription auth is selected, scrub OPENAI_API_KEY and other API-key env vars from the child process and assert this in tests.
If a private OpenAI-compatible bridge is needed for Eve, keep it loopback/private and prove the endpoint cannot be reached publicly.
If a direct AI SDK LanguageModel adapter over app-server is practical, prove generateText with a minimal prompt and sanitized output.
If no supported path exists, mark this task blocked and do not proceed to Eve replacement.
mandatoryVerification: Flat meaningful Effect.gen programs for primary runtime operations.
Typed errors handled in .pipe(...) with catchTag, catchTags, or mapError.
Canonical Effect Schema contracts and schema-derived types from @bundjil/codex-oauth.
No unsafe casts, local DTO mirrors, manual object readers/mappers, or trivial wrappers/helpers.
App-server startup, handshake, auth strategy, API-key env scrubbing, and failure mapping are tested without network where possible.
Live proof logs contain no access tokens, refresh tokens, authorization codes, raw OAuth responses, private prompts, or full request/response bodies.
Provider errors are mapped to safe tagged errors.
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-oauth test
bun run check-types
completionCriteria: A sanitized live proof records the selected runtime path.
The spec and package README are updated with the result.
A failure to prove support blocks Eve model replacement rather than adding a fallback fake.

tasks.title: Define @bundjil/codex-runtime app-server services
tasks.description: Create the package contract for starting, isolating, and speaking to Codex app-server without wiring Eve's default model yet.
implementationNotes: Use package layout src/schemas.ts, src/errors.ts, src/auth-strategy.ts, src/app-server-protocol.ts, src/app-server-client.service.ts, src/app-server-process.layer.ts, and src/index.ts.
Model auth strategies explicitly: subscription via isolated CODEX_HOME, API key BYOK, and test/mock.
Subscription mode must remove OPENAI_API_KEY, CODEX_API_KEY, and other API-key env vars from the child process unless a task explicitly proves they are harmless.
Do not expose an HTTP bridge or change apps/agent in this task.
mandatoryVerification: Flat meaningful Effect.gen programs for process startup, protocol calls, and shutdown.
Typed errors handled in .pipe(...) with catchTag, catchTags, or mapError.
Canonical Effect Schema contracts and schema-derived types from @bundjil/codex-runtime.
No unsafe casts, local DTO mirrors, manual object readers/mappers, or trivial wrappers/helpers.
Tests cover app-server request/response encoding, auth strategy env scrubbing, process startup failure mapping, protocol failure mapping, and safe error redaction.
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-runtime test
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-runtime build
bun run check-types
completionCriteria: @bundjil/codex-runtime exports app-server schemas, errors, service tags, and memory/mock layers.
Package README documents app-server scope, auth strategy, env hygiene, and proof commands.
No Eve app model change is introduced.

tasks.title: Wire Eve to the proven model provider
tasks.description: Add app-owned model-provider selection so apps/agent can choose AI Gateway fallback or the proven Codex-auth-backed provider.
implementationNotes: Keep apps/agent/agent/config.ts as the app-owned config boundary.
Add app-owned model-provider wiring only after prove-codex-auth-runtime-path completes.
Preserve the current Vercel AI Gateway model as a fallback until Codex provider proof is stable.
If using @ai-sdk/openai-compatible, install and configure it only after the runtime endpoint is proven private and compatible.
mandatoryVerification: Eve agent model remains an AI SDK-compatible LanguageModel or Gateway model string.
Flat meaningful Effect.gen programs for provider selection and config parsing where fallible.
Typed errors handled in .pipe(...) with catchTag, catchTags, or mapError.
Canonical Effect Schema contracts and schema-derived types from owning packages.
No unsafe casts, local DTO mirrors, manual object readers/mappers, or trivial wrappers/helpers.
bun run --filter @bundjil/agent test
bun run --filter @bundjil/agent build
bun run check-types
Local /eve/v1/info verifies the selected model/provider metadata without exposing secrets.
completionCriteria: apps/agent can select AI Gateway fallback or Codex provider through Effect Config.
No provider secret is committed or logged.
App README documents the provider selection and fallback behavior.

tasks.title: Add a Vercel-compatible KeyValueStore adapter
tasks.description: Add an adapter for a Vercel Marketplace KV provider, expected to be Upstash Redis, behind Effect KeyValueStore.
implementationNotes: Do not use @vercel/kv; Vercel KV is sunset for new work.
Prefer Upstash Redis through Vercel Marketplace so env vars are provisioned through the linked project.
Keep the adapter behind KeyValueStore so @bundjil/codex-oauth does not depend on app code.
Decide and document token encryption before storing refresh tokens in hosted KV.
mandatoryVerification: Flat meaningful Effect.gen programs for adapter operations.
Typed errors handled in .pipe(...) with catchTag, catchTags, or mapError.
Canonical Effect Schema contracts and schema-derived types from @bundjil/codex-oauth.
No unsafe casts, local DTO mirrors, manual object readers/mappers, or trivial wrappers/helpers.
Tests use a mock or memory adapter by default; live Upstash tests are opt-in.
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-oauth test
bun run --filter @bundjil/codex-oauth build
bun run check-types
completionCriteria: A KeyValueStore-compatible adapter exists for the chosen Vercel KV provider.
Env vars are parsed with Effect Config and secrets use Config.redacted.
Docs describe provisioning through Vercel Marketplace and local env pull.

tasks.title: Write comprehensive Codex OAuth provider documentation
tasks.description: Update root, architecture, app, and package docs with the final provider boundary, setup commands, verification proof, and security constraints.
implementationNotes: Update README.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, docs/README.md, docs/architecture/eve-agent.md, docs/architecture/effect-patterns.md if generalized, docs/architecture/repo-structure.md, docs/architecture/testing-and-quality.md, apps/agent/README.md, and packages/codex-oauth/README.md.
Docs must mark future work and unsupported paths clearly.
Docs must include exact local commands and sanitized proof shape.
mandatoryVerification: Documentation distinguishes implemented behavior from research, opt-in proof, and future hosted storage work.
Documentation includes production and test call graphs.
Documentation includes the mandatory 3-pass Effect TS implementation audit rule.
No docs include token values, authorization codes, or raw OAuth payloads.
bun run check
bun run verification
completionCriteria: All durable boundaries are documented.
Setup and rollback instructions are clear.
The task ledger records final verification evidence.
